nl.statement: Finite-population Bernoulli randomization design (assignment world): probability comes from the experimenter's independent heterogeneous Bernoulli coin flips over the finite assignment space Ω = (I_n → Bool); the design variable p and its feasible class are the decision objects. — Reuse the finite-sum design layer FiniteDesign (E/Var/Cov as Finset algebra) and the product Bernoulli design bernoulliDesign p (per-unit coin flips, cross-unit independence STRUCTURAL via prodDesign). This directly realizes ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli. bernoulliDesign_E_treat/_E_ctrl give the marginal treatment/control expectations feeding pi_i^1/pi_i^0. The positivity floor epsilon and budget B_n restrict p but do not change the world. Verified signatures: bernoulliDesign (Bernoulli.lean:60), FiniteDesign (Design.lean).

Bipartite interference graph + fixed finite-population potential-outcome schedule: known bipartite graph G_n over I_n × O_n, outcome neighborhoods N_i(G_n), fixed bounded potential outcomes Y_i(z_{N_i}), Hajek exposure estimator and its graph-only conservative variance objects. — Search trace: grepped '## Experimentation' in ../doc/API.md + scanned Causalean/Experimentation/{DesignBased,UnknownInterference}; no BipartiteDesign / bipartite-graph structure exists (all interference substrate is one-mode: ExposureMappingInterference, UnknownInterference.Interferes/InterfDep). So the bipartite graph layer (I_n, O_n, G_n, N_i, M_k, degrees, shared sets S_ij) is new local scaffolding. It nonetheless SITS INSIDE the S1 FiniteDesign world: fixed potential outcomes Y_i as functions of the treatment subvector on N_i mirror UnknownInterference.y / y_eq_of_agree_on_interferers (analogue F3 should follow for the interference-restriction algebra), and the all-treated/all-control exposure indicators T_i/C_i are products over N_i (compare Exposure.expoInd). Define a local BipartiteExperiment structure bundling G_n and the neighborhood accessors.
For every i in O_n and every z,z' in {0,1}^{m_n}, z_{N_i(G_n)} = z'_{N_i(G_n)} implies Y_i(z_{N_i}) = Y_i(z'_{N_i}).

review.note: Quantification over Bool-valued assignment vectors exactly represents z,z' in {0,1}^{m_n}, and equality on every k in N_i implies equality of Yfun, precisely encoding neighborhood SUTVA.

standard.name: bipartite interference / neighborhood SUTVA
standard.citation: Lu, S., Shi, L., Fang, Y., Zhang, W., and Ding, P. (2025). Design-based causal inference in bipartite experiments. arXiv:2501.09844.

nl.statement: For every k in I_n, Z_k is Bernoulli(p_k), and the collection {Z_k : k in I_n} is mutually independent.

review.note: Equality of D with the heterogeneous Bernoulli product design is equivalent to every coordinate having Bernoulli(p_k) law and the coordinate collection being mutually independent; hp0 and hp1 only establish the intrinsic probability domain.

standard.name: independent non-identical Bernoulli randomization
standard.citation: Leung, M. P. (2021). Causal inference under approximate neighborhood interference. arXiv:1911.07085.

nl.statement: For every k in I_n, epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon.

review.note: Unfolding gives exactly the coordinatewise conjunction epsilon <= p_k and p_k <= 1-epsilon for every intervention unit k.

standard.name: uniform overlap for heterogeneous Bernoulli exposure probabilities
standard.citation: Leung, M. P. (2021). Causal inference under approximate neighborhood interference. arXiv:1911.07085.

review.note: The finite Fintype sum over all intervention units is exactly the stated budget identity sum_k p_k = B_n.

nl.statement: For every i in O_n, |Y_i^1| <= 1 and |Y_i^0| <= 1.

review.note: Y1 and Y0 unfold to the all-treated and all-control potential outcomes, and the definition imposes exactly both absolute-value bounds for every outcome unit.

standard.name: bounded finite-population potential outcomes
standard.citation: Lu, S., Shi, L., Fang, Y., Zhang, W., and Ding, P. (2025). Design-based causal inference in bipartite experiments. arXiv:2501.09844.

review.note: The universal bound |N_i|≤dbar is equivalent to max_i d_i≤dbar, while 0<dbar enforces the core symbol's declared intrinsic domain dbar ∈ (0,∞).

standard.name: bounded outcome-side degree in sparse bipartite graphs
standard.citation: Lu, S., Shi, L., Fang, Y., Zhang, W., and Ding, P. (2025). Design-based causal inference in bipartite experiments. arXiv:2501.09844.

review.note: The universal bound |overlapNbrs(i)|≤Dbar is exactly Δ_n≤Dbar when Δ_n is the maximum overlap-dependency degree, and 0<Dbar enforces its declared intrinsic domain.

standard.name: bounded dependency-graph degree
standard.citation: Aronow, P. M. and Samii, C. (2017). Estimating average causal effects under general interference, with application to a social network experiment. Annals of Applied Statistics, 11(4), 1912-1947.

review.note: For a real sequence, liminf_n σ²_n>0 is equivalent to the existence of c>0 such that σ²_n≥c eventually.

standard.name: nondegenerate asymptotic design variance
standard.citation: Lu, S., Shi, L., Fang, Y., Zhang, W., and Ding, P. (2025). Design-based causal inference in bipartite experiments. arXiv:2501.09844.

nl.statement: P_{n,B_n,epsilon} = {p in [0,1]^{m_n} : epsilon <= p_k <= 1-epsilon for every k in I_n, and sum_{k in I_n} p_k = B_n}
nl.frozen_body:
Under Assumptions~\ref{obj:ass:positivity-floor} and~\ref{obj:ass:budget-balance}, with \(0<\epsilon<1/2\), define
\[
\mathcal P_{n,B_n,\epsilon}
=
\left\{
p\in[0,1]^{m_n}:
\epsilon\le p_k\le 1-\epsilon\ \text{for every } k\in I_n,
\quad
\sum_{k\in I_n}p_k=B_n
\right\}.
\]

review.note: Unfolding the fields gives exactly p_k ∈ [0,1], ε ≤ p_k ≤ 1−ε for every k, and ∑_k p_k = B; EpsilonAdmissible merely enforces the globally declared domain ε ∈ (0,1/2).

nl.statement: D_1(p,Z) = sum_{i in O_n} T_i(Z) / pi_i^1(p), D_0(p,Z) = sum_{i in O_n} C_i(Z) / pi_i^0(p)

review.note: After unfolding expT, expC, piT, and piC, the two product components are exactly D₁(p,Z)=∑_i T_i(Z)/π_i¹(p) and D₀(p,Z)=∑_i C_i(Z)/π_i⁰(p).

nl.statement: hat_tau_H(p) = 1{D_1(p,Z) > 0}{sum_{i in O_n} T_i(Z) Y_i^obs / pi_i^1(p)} / D_1(p,Z) - 1{D_0(p,Z) > 0}{sum_{i in O_n} C_i(Z) Y_i^obs / pi_i^0(p)} / D_0(p,Z)

review.note: After unfolding the denominators, exposures, exposure probabilities, and observed outcome Yfun i z, each if-branch is exactly the corresponding indicator-weighted Hájek ratio with the stated zero-denominator convention.
Unfolding expT, expC, piT, piC, Y1, Y0, mu1, and mu0 yields exactly the paper's two centered inverse-exposure terms with the same subtraction and signs.

nl.statement: sigma_{G_n,p}^2(Y) = n Var_p(n^(-1) sum_{i in O_n} eta_i(p,Z)) = n^(-1) sum_{i,j in O_n} E_p[eta_i(p,Z) eta_j(p,Z)]
nl.frozen_body:
For a working finite design $D$, define
\[
\sigma^2_{G_n,D,p}(Y)
=
n\,\operatorname{Var}_D\!\left(
\frac{1}{n}\sum_{i\in O_n}\eta_i(p,Z)
\right).
\]
When $D$ is the heterogeneous Bernoulli design indexed by $p$, abbreviate this quantity as $\sigma^2_{G_n,p}(Y)$.

review.note: The Lean definition is exactly n Var_D(n⁻¹∑η_i) for an arbitrary working finite design D. Under the heterogeneous Bernoulli specialization D = bernoulliDesign p, the paper abbreviates this as σ²_{G_n,p}(Y). The raw n⁻¹∑_{i,j} E_p[η_iη_j] expansion additionally uses centering and belongs to the later variance theorem, not this definition.

nl.statement: V_env(G_n,p) = 4 n^(-1) sum_{i,j in O_n} {r_{ij}^1(G_n,p) + r_{ij}^0(G_n,p) + 2 r_{ij}^{10}(G_n)}

review.note: After unfolding r1, r0, and r10, Lean has exactly 4 n⁻¹ times the ordered double sum of r_ij^1+r_ij^0+2r_ij^10.

nl.statement: p_n^*(G_n) in argmin_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} V_env(G_n,p), whenever the argmin set is nonempty

review.note: When the argmin is nonempty, h.choose selects exactly a feasible global minimizer of varEnvelope; the fallback only totalizes the selector in the case where the paper imposes no requirement.

nl.statement: g_k(G_n,p) = partial_{p_k}(V_env(G_n,p)/4) = n^(-1) sum_{i,j in O_n : k in S_{ij}(G_n)} {-(product_{ell in S_{ij}(G_n)} p_ell^(-1)) p_k^(-1) + (product_{ell in S_{ij}(G_n)} (1-p_ell)^(-1)) (1-p_k)^(-1)}

review.note: Unfolding shared = N_i ∩ N_j, the if-filtered double sum is exactly the restricted sum over ordered pairs with k ∈ S_ij, and its two terms are the coordinate derivative of V_env/4.
The definition unfolds directly to hat_V_cons(G_n,p) = V_env(G_n,p).

nl.statement: p_n^{deg}(G_n) in argmin_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} sum_{k in I_n} h_k(G_n){p_k^(-1) + (1-p_k)^(-1)}, whenever the argmin set is nonempty

review.note: When the feasible surrogate argmin is nonempty, h.choose selects a feasible point minimizing the unfolded objective ∑_k h_k(G_n)(p_k⁻¹ + (1-p_k)⁻¹); the fallback only totalizes the paper's unspecified empty-argmin branch.

nl.statement: alpha_cert(G_n) = V_env(G_n,p_n^{deg}(G_n)) / min_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} V_env(G_n,p) when min_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} V_env(G_n,p) > 0, and alpha_cert(G_n) = 1 when min_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} V_env(G_n,p) = 0 (no-loss convention: a zero envelope minimum forces V_env(G_n,p_n^{deg}(G_n))=0 as well, since V_env is a nonnegative sum over shared-neighborhood pairs that vanishes for every feasible design precisely when there are no such pairs, so there is no relative loss). Thus alpha_cert(G_n) is a total, [1,infty)-valued observable on the admitted bounded-degree graph class.
nl.frozen_body:
Define
\[
\alpha_{\mathrm{cert}}(G_n)=
\begin{cases}
\dfrac{V_{\mathrm{env}}\!\left(G_n,p_n^{\deg}(G_n)\right)}
{V_{\mathrm{env}}\!\left(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)\right)},
& \text{if }0<V_{\mathrm{env}}\!\left(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)\right),\\[1.2em]
1,
& \text{otherwise}.
\end{cases}
\]
Here, each design selector equals its respective feasible minimizer when one exists, and equals the homogeneous budget vector otherwise.

review.note: On the admitted nonempty feasible class envMin is the nonnegative envelope minimum, so the Lean positive branch is exactly the stated ratio and its else branch is exactly the zero-minimum no-loss convention.

nl.statement: Under ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, if p_k = p for every k in I_n for a common scalar p in (0,1), then r_{ij}^1(G_n,p) = 1{S_{ij}(G_n) != emptyset}(p^(-|S_{ij}(G_n)|) - 1), r_{ij}^0(G_n,p) = 1{S_{ij}(G_n) != emptyset}((1-p)^(-|S_{ij}(G_n)|) - 1), r_{ij}^{10}(G_n) = 1{S_{ij}(G_n) != emptyset}, and sigma_{G_n,p}^2(Y) = n^(-1) sum_{i,j in O_n} [r_{ij}^1(G_n,p)(Y_i^1-mu_1)(Y_j^1-mu_1) + r_{ij}^0(G_n,p)(Y_i^0-mu_0)(Y_j^0-mu_0) + 2 r_{ij}^{10}(G_n)(Y_i^1-mu_1)(Y_j^0-mu_0)], which is the homogeneous Bernoulli Hajek overlap formula of Lu, Shi, Fang, Zhang, and Ding (2025).

review.note: After unfolding shared, r1, r0, r10, IndepHeteroBernoulli, and varScale, the Lean statement is mathematically equivalent to the paper: nonempty shared neighborhoods are exactly positive-cardinality ones, inverse natural powers equal the stated negative powers under 0 < pc, and the omitted syntactic r10 conjunct holds definitionally as the shared-neighborhood indicator.

nl.statement: Under ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli and ass:bounded-outcomes, for every p in (0,1)^{m_n}, E_p[eta_i(p,Z) eta_j(p,Z)] = r_{ij}^1(G_n,p)(Y_i^1-mu_1)(Y_j^1-mu_1) + r_{ij}^0(G_n,p)(Y_i^0-mu_0)(Y_j^0-mu_0) + r_{ij}^{10}(G_n){(Y_i^1-mu_1)(Y_j^0-mu_0) + (Y_i^0-mu_0)(Y_j^1-mu_1)} for every i,j in O_n. Consequently sigma_{G_n,p}^2(Y) = n^(-1) sum_{i,j in O_n} [r_{ij}^1(G_n,p)(Y_i^1-mu_1)(Y_j^1-mu_1) + r_{ij}^0(G_n,p)(Y_i^0-mu_0)(Y_j^0-mu_0) + r_{ij}^{10}(G_n){(Y_i^1-mu_1)(Y_j^0-mu_0) + (Y_i^0-mu_0)(Y_j^1-mu_1)}] <= V_env(G_n,p).

review.note: After unfolding linScore, r1/r0/r10, varScale, varEnvelope, IndepHeteroBernoulli, and BoundedOutcomes, the pairwise identity and envelope bound match exactly; the paper’s displayed intermediate variance equality follows from the pairwise identity because the Bernoulli hypothesis makes every linScore mean zero, while hp0/hp1 are redundant consequences of hpos/hlt.

nl.statement: The feasible set P_{n,B_n,epsilon} is a nonempty compact convex subset of [0,1]^{m_n}, the map p -> V_env(G_n,p) is convex on P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, and therefore the argmin set in def:optimal-design is nonempty. For every minimizer p_n^*(G_n) there exist lambda_n in R and box multipliers nu_{k,n}^+ >= 0, nu_{k,n}^- >= 0 (k in I_n) such that the observable KKT system g_k(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) = lambda_n - nu_{k,n}^+ + nu_{k,n}^- holds for each k in I_n, together with nu_{k,n}^+((p_n^*(G_n))_k-(1-epsilon)) = 0 and nu_{k,n}^-(epsilon-(p_n^*(G_n))_k) = 0.

review.note: After unfolding feasibleSet/FeasibleDesign, varEnvelope, and envelopeGrad, Lean states the same nonempty compact convex feasible class, convex envelope, argmin existence, and universal KKT certificate; the explicit ε and budget-domain hypotheses are intrinsic setup conditions, and the lower-face complementary-slackness sign is exactly the paper's equivalent zero identity.

nl.statement: Under ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, ass:bounded-outcome-degree, and ass:bounded-overlap-dependency, sup_{p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} P_p(D_1(p,Z) = 0 or D_0(p,Z) = 0) = O(n^{-1}).

review.note: After unfolding the feasible-design class, Hájek denominators, degree predicates, and Bernoulli design, the eventual uniform bound over every feasible q is equivalent to the stated supremum being O(n^{-1}); admissible ε/B and card(O_n)=n are standing domain bookkeeping.

nl.statement: Under ass:bipartite-interference, ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli, ass:bounded-outcomes, ass:bounded-outcome-degree, ass:bounded-overlap-dependency, and ass:variance-nondegenerate, sqrt(n){hat_tau_H(p_n^*(G_n)) - tau_n} = n^(-1/2) sum_{i in O_n} eta_i(p_n^*(G_n),Z) + o_p(1), and sqrt(n){hat_tau_H(p_n^*(G_n)) - tau_n} / sqrt(sigma_{G_n,p_n^*(G_n)}^2(Y)) ->d N(0,1).

review.note: Unfolded every def reached from the statement: `HeteroLinearization` = ∀δ>0, Pr(|√(card Ox)·(τ̂_H(p)−τ) − (√(card Ox))⁻¹·∑ᵢ linScore| ≥ δ) → 0, i.e. exactly the paper's `√n{τ̂_H(p*)−τ_n} = n^{-1/2}∑ᵢη_i(p*,Z) + o_p(1)` once `hcardEq : ∀ᶠ n, card (Ox n) = n` identifies the paper's stage index with |O_n| (O_n = {1..n} in the core symbol table, so this is the paper's own setup, and it is the weaker `∀ᶠ` form, so it does not strengthen the hypothesis); `linScore` = (T_i/π_i¹−1)(Y_i¹−μ₁) − (C_i/π_i⁰−1)(Y_i⁰−μ₀) = η_i verbatim; `hajekEstimator` = the two-arm ratio with the paper's explicit zero-denominator convention; `tau` = μ₁−μ₀; `varScale D p` = card O · Var(card O⁻¹ ∑ η) = σ²_{G_n,p}(Y) verbatim (def:variance-scale). Second conjunct is pointwise CDF convergence to `stdNormalCdf` at every s, which for the (everywhere-continuous) N(0,1) CDF is logically equivalent to →d N(0,1) — matched in both directions, not a weaker witness form. Hypotheses map 1-1 onto the note's list: hBI=ass:bipartite-interference, hBern=ass:independent-heterogeneous-bernoulli (D = bernoulliDesign p), hbdd=ass:bounded-outcomes, hdeg=ass:bounded-outcome-degree, hdep=ass:bounded-overlap-dependency, hnd=VarianceNondegenerate = ∃c>0, ∀ᶠ n, c ≤ σ²_n, which is exactly liminf σ² > 0. The extra premises are all standing-setup/regularity bookkeeping, not crux laundering: hfeas/hε realize ass:positivity-floor + ass:budget-balance (the paper's design class P_{n,B_n,ε}, which p_n^*(G_n) belongs to by definition of the argmin), hp0/hp1 are the [0,1] box implied by feasibility, hstar : p n = optimalDesign pins the design to the paper's p_n^*(G_n), and hεfloor (a uniform positive floor on the ε-sequence) is vacuously satisfied by the paper's fixed constant ε ∈ (0,1/2) — it only generalizes ε to an array. The former denominator-tightness and linear-score-CLT gate hypotheses are gone: both are derived in-proof (`heteroDenominatorTightness_discharged`, `hetero_linscore_clt_of_depgraph`), so no rate/bound the paper derives is assumed. Hypotheses are jointly satisfiable (the junk-fallback branch of `optimalDesign` is itself feasible under BudgetAdmissible), so the claim is not vacuous. Proof completeness is out of scope.

nl.statement: Under the assumptions of thm:hetero-clt, hat_V_cons(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) = V_env(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) is conservative for sigma_{G_n,p_n^*(G_n)}^2(Y), and for every sequence of potential-outcome schedules satisfying those assumptions, liminf_{n -> infty} P_{p_n^*(G_n)}(tau_n in [hat_tau_H(p_n^*(G_n)) - z_{1-alpha_cov/2} sqrt{hat_V_cons(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) / n}, hat_tau_H(p_n^*(G_n)) + z_{1-alpha_cov/2} sqrt{hat_V_cons(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) / n}]) >= 1-alpha_cov.

review.note: After unfolding varEstCons, varScale, hajekEstimator, optimalDesign, FeasibleDesign, and the CLT assumptions, Lean states the same variance domination and Wald liminf coverage; the absolute-value event is equivalent to interval membership, and eventual card(Ox n)=n makes the card-based normalization asymptotically identical to the paper's n.

nl.statement: For every epsilon in (0,1/2), every c_disp > 0, and every C_disp >= 1, there is a sequence of finite bipartite graphs satisfying the stated positive-energy, degree-dispersion, and h-weight-ratio bounds for which alpha_cert(G_n) tends to infinity. Thus those first-order dispersion summaries do not uniformly bound the surrogate approximation ratio.

review.note: After unfolding EpsilonAdmissible, BudgetAdmissible, sdeg, hWeight, and approxRatio, the Lean guards are equivalent to positive degree energy, the normalized maximum-degree bound, and the positive-h-weight max/min ratio bound; Tendsto atTop atTop is divergence to infinity, and the eventual guards are equivalent here because the existential sequence may be reindexed past the finite exceptional prefix.

nl.statement: Set rho = B_n / m_n and let p^hom denote the homogeneous feasible design with (p^hom)_k = rho for every k in I_n; assume rho in (epsilon, 1-epsilon) and rho != 1/2. If the homogeneous-point envelope-gradient scores {g_k(G_n, p^hom) : k in I_n} of def:kkt-gradient are not all equal, then p^hom is not a minimizer of p -> V_env(G_n,p) over P_{n,B_n,epsilon}; consequently every envelope-optimal design p_n^*(G_n) of def:optimal-design is non-homogeneous and satisfies the strict inequality V_env(G_n, p_n^*(G_n)) < V_env(G_n, p^hom), and the gap admits the explicit observable lower bound V_env(G_n,p^hom) - V_env(G_n,p_n^*(G_n)) >= 2 Delta_g min{eta_box, Delta_g / L_ab}, where Delta_g = max_{k in I_n} g_k(G_n,p^hom) - min_{k in I_n} g_k(G_n,p^hom) > 0 is the gradient-score spread, eta_box = min{rho - epsilon, 1 - epsilon - rho} > 0, a in argmax_{k in I_n} g_k(G_n,p^hom), b in argmin_{k in I_n} g_k(G_n,p^hom), and L_ab = sup_{q in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}} (e_b - e_a)^T Hess(V_env(G_n,cdot)/4)(q) (e_b - e_a) is the directional second-order modulus of the graph-only envelope along the budget-feasible direction e_b - e_a (all four quantities being observable functions of G_n, epsilon, and B_n), with the bound read as 2 Delta_g eta_box when L_ab = 0. In particular, for singleton-exposure graphs, in which each outcome unit's neighborhood is a single intervention unit so that every nonempty S_{ij}(G_n) is a singleton, the gradient scores reduce to observable per-unit degree summaries, and the hypothesis (hence the strict improvement of the optimal heterogeneous design over the homogeneous design) holds whenever those summaries are not all equal.

review.note: Unfolding SeparationConclusion, feasibleSet, envelopeGrad, varEnvelope, sdeg, and dirModulus gives the same non-minimizer claim, universal strict improvement and explicit gap for every feasible envelope minimizer, directional Hessian modulus with the L_ab=0 convention, and singleton formula n⁻¹s_k²((1−ρ)⁻²−ρ⁻²); the standalone unequal-degree trigger is exactly the paper’s singleton consequence.

nl.statement: Under ass:bounded-outcome-degree, which forces max_{i,j in O_n} |S_{ij}(G_n)| <= dbar, with the explicit constant C(epsilon, dbar) = max{1, epsilon^{-(dbar-1)}} depending only on epsilon and dbar, the observable approximation ratio of def:approximation-ratio satisfies alpha_cert(G_n) <= C(epsilon, dbar). The bound follows from a uniform sandwich, valid for every feasible p in P_{n,B_n,epsilon}, between the graph-only envelope V_env(G_n,p) of def:graph-envelope and the additive degree-surrogate objective sum_{k in I_n} h_k(G_n){p_k^{-1} + (1-p_k)^{-1}} minimized in def:surrogate-design, in which the per-pair product loads over the shared-neighborhood sets S_{ij}(G_n) are comparable to their additive per-coordinate counterparts up to a multiplicative factor controlled by the bounded shared-neighborhood size dbar and the positivity floor epsilon. This is the positive counterpart, in the paper's own sparse bounded-degree regime, of the negative first-order-summary answer proved in oeq:dispersion-certificate.

review.note: After unfolding all referenced definitions, Lean states exactly the paper's uniform A(p) ≤ V_env(p)/4 ≤ C(ε,dbar)A(p) sandwich and α_cert ≤ C(ε,dbar), with C = max{1, ε^{-(dbar-1)}}; ε admissibility, positive dbar, and BudgetAdmissible are intrinsic-domain and standing setup conditions.

nl.statement: Let {X_{n,i}:1<=i<=n} be centered real random variables with a dependency graph of maximum degree at most D, |X_{n,i}|<=M for fixed finite D and M, and Var(sum_i X_{n,i})=v_n with v_n>=c n for some c>0 eventually. Then (sum_i X_{n,i})/sqrt(v_n) converges in distribution to N(0,1).

review.note: DepGraph supplies measurability and the dependency factorization, depSum is the row sum, centering makes the stated second moment v_n equal its variance, and CDF convergence for arbitrary s is equivalent to weak convergence to the continuous N(0,1) law.
